calls.title: My MS_TEAMS Meeting (NWC / AL Shalfa — System Implementation)
calls.summary: Requirements-finalisation session for NWC/AL Shalfa system implementation. Phased approach agreed; QR codes, Arabic description field, asset status dropdown, and client evaluation ratings in WO workflow all confirmed. Mini-UAT scheduled for Thursday.
key_points: Phased implementation approach agreed for development and deployment.
QR code generation for assets confirmed feasible; sticker size specifications to be confirmed with TBC.
Dedicated Arabic description field to be added to the building module.
Asset operational status to be changed from single-line text to a dropdown for better filtering and reporting.
Work order workflow enhanced to include client evaluation (excellent / good / poor ratings).
Client ratings will trigger subsequent actions: auto-closure or initiation of CM task.
Mini-UAT scheduled for Thursday to review implemented changes.
decisions: Phased implementation approach adopted.
Asset operational status: text field → dropdown.
Client evaluation ratings (excellent/good/poor) integrated into WO workflow.

action_items.task: Confirm sticker size specifications with TBC
Implement Arabic description field in building module
Implement client evaluation ratings in WO workflow
Conduct mini-UAT

open_items: TBC sticker size confirmation; mini-UAT readiness by Thursday.

calls.title: Following: Daily Facilio CAFM Workshops | Resolving Issues
calls.summary: Working session on CIT CAFM issues. PPM bulk update not supported; moving to Excel import with training. Inventory description fix targeted 15 May. MOC project onboarding to OCI instance agreed in principle; pricing and licensing to be finalised.
key_points: PPM upload functionality requires revision — moving to Excel import with a training session for data cleanup.
Bulk updating PPM schedules for numerous assets by location or type presents a significant challenge with current UI.
Facilio AI accuracy issue to be checked with Sudarshan.
Inventory management issues: inaccurate item descriptions and poor searchability; fix to display descriptions alongside item codes planned by 15 May.
Investigating possibility of updating inventory against closed work orders and adjusting current stock levels.
MOC project: recommended to onboard within OCI instance; pricing and implementation timelines to be finalised.
User licensing for MOC needs clarification (comparing existing Facilio users vs. required 100+ seats).
Daily follow-up calls scheduled to track progress.
decisions: PPM to move to Excel import method with training session for data cleanup.
Inventory fix (descriptions alongside item codes) targeted by 15 May.
MOC project to be onboarded within OCI instance.
Daily follow-up calls established.

action_items.task: Revise PPM upload to Excel import and schedule training session
Check Facilio AI accuracy issue
Fix inventory item descriptions + searchability
Investigate inventory update against closed WOs
Finalise MOC pricing and implementation timeline
Clarify MOC user licensing requirements

risks: PPM bulk update capability not yet available. Inventory descriptions inaccurate. AI accuracy concern raised. MOC scope and licensing unresolved.
open_items: PPM Excel import tool; inventory fix by 15 May; MOC licensing clarification; Sudarshan AI check.

calls.title: CWRC — Access Control Parkomax Integration
calls.summary: Integration scoping session for CWRC resident access control between Facilio and ParcoMax. Architecture agreed: two-way resident data sync, one-way gym subscription push. ParcoMax API license required but cost unconfirmed — a potential procurement blocker.
key_points: Facilio will push resident data to ParcoMax; ParcoMax returns resident IDs and access card numbers.
Facility access (dining, gym) permissions to be pushed from Facilio to ParcoMax.
Gym subscription status pushed from ParcoMax to Facilio (triggered by POS); Facilio stores status, not payment data.
Two-way integration agreed for resident data; one-way push for subscription status.
Resident check-out triggers Facilio API call to ParcoMax for user deactivation.
ParcoMax requires an API license for integration; costs to be confirmed.
Master data (contractors, departments) managed via ParcoMax APIs; Facilio creates new entries if needed.
Transactional data (daily movements) not integrated to avoid duplication.
decisions: Two-way integration for resident data; one-way push for subscription status.
Facilio calls ParcoMax API on resident check-out to deactivate user.

action_items.task: Confirm API license cost for integration
Finalise integration API specifications with ParcoMax

risks: ParcoMax API license cost unconfirmed — potential cost and procurement blocker for integration.
open_items: ParcoMax API license cost confirmation; API specification documentation from ParcoMax.

calls.title: Facilio <> MAF — Support Transition
calls.summary: Formal support transition call for Maf-tilal-algaf and Maf-alzahir to Sleo's support team with Mithun as primary contact. Support via Freshdesk; severity SLAs established. User adoption challenges with new service providers flagged; train-the-trainer model agreed.
key_points: Maf-tilal-algaf and Maf-alzahir officially transitioned to Sleo's support team; Mithun is primary contact.
Support requests to be submitted via support@facilio.com, managed through Freshdesk.
Dedicated POC recommended from MAF side to filter and submit issues to avoid duplicates.
Ticket categories defined: product defects, implementation issues/requests, user adoption/education, roadmap items.
Severity levels P1/P2/P3 and acknowledgment SLAs established.
Challenges with user adoption and training new service providers raised.
Facilio to support under a train-the-trainer model.
Freshdesk access to be provided to MAF admins for ticket tracking.
decisions: Support transition to Sleo effective immediately; Mithun is primary contact.
Dedicated MAF POC to be appointed to filter support requests.
Freshdesk access to be granted to MAF admins.

action_items.task: Appoint dedicated POC for support ticket submission
Provide Freshdesk access to MAF admins
Support user adoption and training for new service providers

risks: User adoption challenges noted for new service providers — train-the-trainer model agreed as mitigation.
open_items: MAF POC appointment; Freshdesk access provisioning.

calls.title: CWRC — Contract Discussion
calls.summary: Contract scope clarification for CWRC. Real-time forecasting excluded; historical bid-price dashboard agreed. Arabic support and contract expiry notifications to be built. Legal team meeting to be scheduled for contract structure discussion.
key_points: Internal legal meeting to be scheduled to discuss contract structure.
Customer inquiry response requires manual upload of date, time, and version number for client behaviour analysis.
Version history restoration accepted as manual attachment of previous proposals.
Real-time market response and predictive forecasting excluded; Facilio will provide historical bid-price dashboards.
Dashboard to display average bid prices and forecast based on vacant bids and contract expirations.
Arabic language support and editing privileges for fixed contract terms to be incorporated.
Automated notifications for contract expiry to be implemented for contract and finance departments.
decisions: Real-time/predictive forecasting excluded from scope.
Dashboard to provide average bid prices and vacancy-based forecast.
Arabic language support and contract term editing to be incorporated.
Automated contract expiry notifications agreed.

action_items.task: Schedule internal legal meeting on contract structure
Implement Arabic language support in contract module
Build average bid-price dashboard with vacancy-based forecast
Implement automated contract expiry notifications

risks: Legal team involvement in contract structure still to be coordinated.
open_items: Internal legal meeting; Arabic language implementation; dashboard build.

calls.title: MODON — Planner Verification
calls.summary: MODON planner verification session. 31 open tasks confirmed; several reopened due to incomplete information, risking BRD submission timeline. Decision to schedule individual 30-minute entity meetings to resolve each item.
key_points: Discrepancy in open task counts resolved after filter adjustments: 31 open tasks confirmed.
Several tasks reopened due to incomplete information or outstanding queries, impacting project timelines.
Issues identified: vendor/subcontractor restriction rules, Oracle integration budget confirmation, missing module fault codes/inspection values.
Decision to export all comments from open tasks and schedule individual 30-minute clarification meetings per entity.
Jhimlee or Renka to lead meetings; Ashwin Bhat Furgo as optional attendee.
Goal is to resolve all pending issues before BRD submission.
Current task update and notification process deemed insufficient.
decisions: Export all comments from open tasks for review.
Schedule individual 30-minute clarification meetings per entity.
Jhimlee / Renka to lead each meeting.

action_items.task: Export all open task comments and schedule individual entity meetings
Resolve vendor/subcontractor restriction rules
Confirm Oracle integration budget
Add missing module fault codes and inspection values

risks: 31 open tasks with reopened items risking BRD submission deadline. Notification process inadequate — tasks not being tracked effectively.
open_items: Oracle integration budget confirmation; fault codes/inspection values to be added; individual entity meetings to be completed.

calls.title: ENEC — Weekly Cadence Call
calls.summary: ENEC workspace management cadence call. Client raised clear concerns about system value without SAP/Employee Central and Outlook integrations. Facilio to share architectural diagrams; follow-up meeting to finalise integration strategy within days.
key_points: BRD covers office space management: desk and room management, parking, deliveries, and lockers.
Critical need for integration with HR systems (SAP/Employee Central) for employee data and offboarding workflows.
Outlook integration essential for meeting room bookings and analytics.
Participants expressed concerns about the system's value without these integrations and automated workflows.
Requirement for searching employees by name to locate assigned desks.
Automated notifications for desk availability and employee departures are crucial.
Architectural diagrams and integration capabilities documentation to be shared by Facilio.
Follow-up meeting scheduled to finalise integration strategies and timelines.
decisions: Facilio to share architectural diagrams and integration capability documentation.
Follow-up meeting scheduled to finalise integration approach within days.

action_items.task: Share architectural diagrams and integration capability documentation
Schedule and conduct follow-up meeting on integration strategy

risks: Client stated the system has limited value without SAP/Employee Central and Outlook integrations. Risk of scope or expectation misalignment if integrations cannot be confirmed promptly.
open_items: SAP/Employee Central integration feasibility; Outlook integration approach; follow-up meeting scheduling.

calls.title: Lunds & Byerlys Touchbase
calls.summary: Multiple issues discussed for Lunds & Byerlys: mobile app inspection visibility, asset decommissioning/refrigerant compliance gap, T&M contract un-expiry not supported, predictive alarm noise. Team working through items collaboratively.
key_points: Mobile app inspection visibility issue for certain users attributed to user permissions and site assignments.
WO asset filtering bug resolved.
Exporting job plan tasks still in progress.
Decommissioning sites/assets: challenges in properly handling refrigerant assets to prevent errors; better integration between asset management and refrigerant compliance needed.
Un-expiring Time & Materials contracts not currently supported — new contracts must be created.
Duplicate data entry required for assigning categories to contracts and provider associations; needs streamlining.
Mobile app connectivity issues and incorrect refrigerant value rounding impact usability.
Predictive alarm rules for condenser approach need refinement (fan speed, ambient temperature, site-specific variations).
decisions: New contracts to be created when T&M contracts expire (un-expiry not supported).

action_items.task: Resolve mobile app inspection visibility permission issue
Complete job plan task export functionality
Improve integration between asset decommissioning and refrigerant compliance
Streamline contract category/provider association data entry
Fix mobile app connectivity and refrigerant value rounding
Refine condenser approach predictive alarm rules

risks: T&M contract un-expiry not supported — operational workaround required. Decommissioning/refrigerant compliance integration gap. Predictive alarms may generate noise due to insufficient data granularity.
open_items: Job plan export; decommissioning/refrigerant integration; alarm rule refinement.
